MASTER CALENDAR JURY

MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF RAYMOND DIGIACOMO's Motion To Amend Complaint To Bring In A New Party Mydatt Services, Inc.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's unopposed "motion to amend complaint to bring in new party defendant Mydatt Services, Inc." is granted.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-06-2020 Set For Trial On Nov-15-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to Amended ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-25-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF RAYMOND DIGIACOMO'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's demurrer to defendant's amended answer is overruled and the motion to strike is denied. An affirmative defense shall be pleaded with the same particularity as a cause of action. (See FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384.) Here, defendant's answer is factually rich and pleads facts that support the affirmative defenses. Plaintiff's arguments regarding the verifications fail as the amended answer is verified, and there is no contention that defendant is acting in bad faith. (See League of Women Voters v. Eu (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 649, 656 ["'The object of a verification is to assure good faith in the averments or statements of a party. [Citations.]' Where, as here, there is no allegation of counsel's bad faith, a claim of inadequate verification is without merit."].) Plaintiff's other arguments also lack merit.

Single order signed as to both demurrer and motion to strike.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-25-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF RAYMOND DIGIACOMO'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's demurrer to defendant's amended answer is overruled and the motion to strike is denied. An affirmative defense shall be pleaded with the same particularity as a cause of action. (See FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384.) Here, defendant's answer is factually rich and pleads facts that support the affirmative defenses. Plaintiff's arguments regarding the verifications fail as there is no contention that defendant is acting in bad faith. (See League of Women Voters v. Eu (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 649, 656 ["'The object of a verification is to assure good faith in the averments or statements of a party. [Citations.]' Where, as here, there is no allegation of counsel's bad faith, a claim of inadequate verification is without merit."].) Plaintiff's other arguments also lack merit.

Single order signed as to both demurrer and motion to strike.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
